title: Rei and Seele
wikitext:
[]: Rei & Seele             Quote Post by kosure » Tue Aug 10, 2004 2:48 pm Okay. This is a bit of a continuation of the Kaworu & Seele topic. But I didn't feel like talking about Rei in such a thread was good, so here we are. In case you're not following the Kaworu & Seele thread, Its basically about Kaworu, and why Seele wanted to send him to NERV. Since Kaworu is a physical extention of Adam, and he had the power to incite third impact, then Rei as an extention of Lillith must have that same power. So my question is this; What was Seele planning on doing with Rei? The way I see it there are Four poeple/groups that are trying to, or have the ability to control third impact; the Angels, Gendou, and Kaworu & Rei. We kind of know how Seele was trying to control or keep tabs on the Angels, and Gendou, and Kaworu, but what about Rei? Maybe they thought that Gendou had enough control over her? Maybe they didn't ever see her as a threat. Either way they should have paid more attention to her. What are your thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Tue Aug 10, 2004 2:59 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Rei's origins are largely contested. The general consensus is that Rei was the unintended byproduct of the failed salvage attempt of Yui Ikari. This is confirmed by dialogue in the series, as well as other sources such as the Red Cross Book. She is not a clone in the sense you are thinking. Rei was an accident, and was later found to be of use and purpose. It is my belief that Rei was created from the body of Lilith, just as Lilith's mass was used to create Eva Unit 01. When Yui was taken into the Eva, her DNA in some way transfered between the Eva Unit and Lilith, who at that time most likely were still attached. The DNA cause Lilith to produce the Rei vessels which are seen about her mid-section.

[]:             Quote Post by kosure » Thu Aug 12, 2004 10:32 am Thats really nice but I'm not talking about how they were created. I'm talking about what Rei had to do with third impact. And what (if anything) Seele was doing to make sure she didn't mess up their plans.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Thu Aug 12, 2004 12:13 pm My point was: Rei was created by accident, she didn't directly have anything to do with Third Impact. Gendo realized he could use her in his plans to begin his own version of Third Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by kosure » Thu Aug 12, 2004 2:52 pm So you're saying that Seele didn't know that Rei was anything but a normal kid?

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Thu Aug 12, 2004 2:54 pm kosure wrote: So you're saying that Seele didn't know that Rei was anything but a normal kid?

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Thu Aug 12, 2004 5:59 pm kosure wrote: So you're saying that Seele didn't know that Rei was anything but a normal kid?

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Thu Aug 12, 2004 6:44 pm Phaze wrote: That's like calling Misato an alien because she has purple hair. It's an anime, and anime characters are known for their eccentric color design.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Thu Aug 12, 2004 7:05 pm When asked about Rei's character design, Yoshiyuki Sadamoto responded, (and I paraphrase) "I felt she didn't have enough striking features, so I gave her red eyes." There you go, no mentioning of being an albino. I'm not saying we should rule out the theory entirely though, perhaps they were meant to be traits of a clone (or maybe Sadamoto just wanted to establish the connection between Kaworu and Rei, through eye color). I still don't think that necessarily makes her an albino. Though the retina is red in color, Rei's eyes appear to maintain their melanin, (of course, she is a cartoon, you don't really see her eyes that well) you can pretty much tell in the EoE, after she merges with Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by aranami » Thu Aug 12, 2004 9:05 pm kosure wrote: So you're saying that Seele didn't know that Rei was anything but a normal kid?

[]:             Quote Post by kosure » Fri Aug 13, 2004 8:59 am the_seventh_child wrote: kosure wrote: So you're saying that Seele didn't know that Rei was anything but a normal kid?

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Fri Aug 13, 2004 9:16 am kosure wrote: the_seventh_child wrote: kosure wrote: So you're saying that Seele didn't know that Rei was anything but a normal kid?

[]:             Quote Post by kosure » Fri Aug 13, 2004 12:05 pm well... :? that was a short thread.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Fri Aug 13, 2004 9:06 pm if they had known about Rei, they would have done something...at most they suspected that she had something to do with gendo...

[]:             Quote Post by RubberSoul » Sat Aug 14, 2004 3:53 am Maybe they wanted to do something with Rei, but they thought Gendo was BSing, so they decided to bring Kaworu in the picture to speed things up.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Aug 14, 2004 1:05 pm aranami wrote: I don't think they knew about her. After she blew herself up seele wanted to question her to find out why she was still alive. If they knew who she was than they would know why she was still alive.
